11208:155391.name: If a display connection faces black screen error due to higher resolution of another connected display, then we DO Not show information icon for res and refresh rate. Information icon showing resolution and refresh rate should not be visible when black screen error is encountered for a specific display e.g., DP port display has black screen --> don't show resolution and refresh rate information icon. Resolution and refresh rate is anyways shown for a DDPM supported monitor.

11208:156269.name: The host device (e.g., laptop or desktop) tries to output a resolution or refresh rate that exceeds the HDMI/DP/USB-C MFDP port cable OR display device's supported bandwidth DDPM GUI shows: Insufficient bandwidth detected. Display resolution or refresh rate has been adjusted to ensure optimal performance. This message can be dismissed using 'X'. It will not auto-dismiss. Once user dismisses the message, it will NOT re-apppear for the continued connection instance i.e., user keeps the display connected. If user disconnects and reconnects the monitor, message will appear again. Device name will continue to appear as expected: 'Other device' OR 'DDPM standard monitor naming convention' Status: 'In use' Note: There is still video data transfer available for display with adjusted resolution. Message will appear separately for each port as applicable
11208:156269.text_content:
The host device (e.g., laptop or desktop) tries to output a resolution or refresh rate that exceeds the HDMI/DP/USB-C MFDP port cable OR display device's supported bandwidth
DDPM GUI shows: Insufficient bandwidth detected. Display resolution or refresh rate has been adjusted to ensure optimal performance.
This message can be dismissed using 'X'. It will not auto-dismiss.
Once user dismisses the message, it will NOT re-apppear for the continued connection instance i.e., user keeps the display connected. If user disconnects and reconnects the monitor, message will appear again.
Device name will continue to appear as expected: 'Other device' OR 'DDPM standard monitor naming convention'
Status: 'In use'
Note: There is still video data transfer available for display with adjusted resolution.
Message will appear separately for each port as applicable
